capabilities: Implement memory prioritization strategies to enhance agent performance and resource management.

procedure.action: Analyze memory usage patterns using memory profiling tools like Valgrind or VisualVM to identify high-consumption areas using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Invoke LRU (Least Recently Used) caching strategy to manage memory for non-critical data following the specified pattern; write a unit test covering the primary code path; assert test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete.

Invoke priority queues to allocate memory resources to high-priority tasks first, leveraging libraries like PriorityBlockingQueue in Java by invoking the target API with the specified parameters; assert response_status < 400 and response_time_ms < 5000; log request_id and response_code.
Set memory usage alerts using monitoring tools like Prometheus when usage exceeds 80% of allocated resources.

Conduct load testing with JMeter to ensure memory prioritization does not degrade performance under high load using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Optimize memory usage in software agents by prioritizing critical tasks and data, ensuring efficient resource allocation and performance.
title: Agent Memory Prioritization in Software Architecture

failureConditions: Excessive memory usage leading to application crashes.
High-priority tasks starved of memory resources; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
Degraded performance under load due to inefficient memory allocation.
metrics: test_coverage >= 0.8 for the modified module before marking complete
successConditions: Memory usage remains below 80% under normal operation; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
High-priority tasks maintain consistent performance metrics; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
System recovers gracefully from memory spikes without crashing; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
